No Poverty.reason: The course mentions 'social responsibility' but lacks direct, explicit content or mechanisms related to poverty alleviation or its specific targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to ending hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to health, well-being, or medical services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (4.3), professional skills for employment (4.4), and explicitly teaches students to identify sustainability issues (4.7).
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.
Students will acquire practical professional skills - problem solving.
奠定會計專業知識與能力 (Students will gain professional accounting knowledge in their field. )

Gender Equality.reason: The course lacks any specific content, objectives, or competencies related to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to affordable, clean energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course equips students with professional skills for employment (Target 8.5) and teaches AIS/ERP systems that enhance economic productivity and efficiency (Target 8.2).
evidence: equip students with a comprehensive understanding of the processes within an accounting system
practical knowledge of key SAP ERP modules

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on AIS and SAP ERP modules directly contributes to upgrading industrial processes (9.4) and enhancing technological capabilities in sectors (9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's core competency of identifying 'social responsibility' and 'ethics' offers a very weak, inferred link to reducing inequalities, but lacks specific content.
evidence: understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to sustainable cities, communities, or urban development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course on AIS/ERP directly supports Target 12.6 by teaching systems to integrate sustainability information into reporting, reinforced by the 'sustainability issues' competency.
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Climate Action.reason: A minimal inferred link exists through the 'sustainability issues' core competency, but the course lacks specific content on climate action or its targets.
evidence: Students will understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to marine ecosystems or life below water.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or life on land.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's 'particular focus on internal controls' directly addresses reducing corruption (16.5) and developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (16.6), reinforced by ethics competency.
evidence: particular focus on the internal controls

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course description, objectives, or schedule relates to global partnerships, resource mobilization, or international cooperation for SDGs.
